Practical example implementations of Loom capabilities

Temporary config file path for isolated tests.

Unit tests for SessionManager — lifecycle, LRU eviction, concurrency.
Create a SessionManager with isolated temp directory.
open() creates a session and returns metadata.
list() returns sessions sorted by last_used_at (newest first).
close() deletes the browser profile directory.
close() returns error dict if session not found.
get_context() returns session metadata if valid.
get_context() returns None if session not found.
Reopening an existing session extends its TTL and returns stable metadata.
LRU eviction triggers when 8+ sessions exist (evicts 9th).
Concurrent close/open on same session name are serialized.
Sessions persist in DB and reload on manager restart.
Unit tests for journey runner — run_journey with fixture playback.  Tests that a
run_journey completes all 23 steps with fixture data.
run_journey generates report.json with step metadata.
run_journey uses fixtures for deterministic offline testing.
Unit tests for URL and input validators (SSRF prevention, gh query sanitization)
Unit tests for loom.server — tool registration and app creation.
Every registered tool name starts with 'research_'.
All promised tool names are registered.
Unit tests for Pydantic parameter models — validation of good/bad inputs.
User agent over 256 chars raises ValidationError.
Proxy without http(s) prefix raises ValidationError.
Any key/value combo passes (validation deferred to config set).
Invalid URL in list raises ValidationError.
Unit tests for ConfigModel — field validation, load/save, atomic writes.
Config load/save and atomic write tests.
load_config reads and merges file over defaults.
load_config uses all defaults if file missing.
load_config merges file values over code defaults.
save_config uses atomic write (uuid tmp + replace).
save_config creates parent directories if needed.
set() updates a config key, validates, and persists.
research_config_set returns error dict on failure.
Comprehensive CLI smoke tests for Loom — all subcommands and functional scenario
Provide a Typer CliRunner for CLI testing.
Help output tests for all subcommands.
Functional tests with mocked MCP calls.
loom config list invokes research_config_get MCP tool.
loom cache stats invokes research_cache_stats MCP tool.
Unit tests for CLI — smoke tests with typer CliRunner.
Provide a Typer CliRunner for CLI testing.
loom fetch with SSRF URL returns non-zero exit.
loom fetch with valid URL invokes MCP tool.
loom config set KEY VALUE works.
Unit tests for CacheStore — atomic writes, UUID tmp, SHA-256, concurrent access.
CacheStore atomic write and concurrent safety tests.
get() returns None for missing keys.
put() and get() store and retrieve data correctly.
put() uses uuid-suffixed tmp file for atomicity.
Concurrent writes to same key do not corrupt data.
Different keys create separate cache files.
get() returns None if cached file has invalid JSON.
Integration tests for MCP roundtrip — spawn server in-process, call tools.  Test
MCP server /tools/list returns all expected tools.
Calling research_fetch through MCP returns expected response.
Calling research_search through MCP returns expected response.
Session management tools work through MCP roundtrip.
MCP layer properly serializes errors from tools.
Unit tests for LLM tools — chat, summarize, extract, classify, translate.  All 8
LLM chat returns structure with text, model, tokens, cost, latency.
LLM extract validates result against provided schema.
LLM classify returns a label from the allow-list.
LLM translate returns a structured result for non-English input.
LLM query expand returns multiple expanded queries.
Unit tests for research_search tool — mocked provider SDKs, normalized output.
research_search tool tests with mocked provider SDKs.
Exa provider returns normalized output with provider field.
Tavily provider returns normalized output with provider field.
include_domains and exclude_domains forwarded to provider.
Unit tests for research_github tool — query sanitization, subprocess mocking, ca
GitHub result is parsed from JSON subprocess output.
GitHub caches results for repeated queries.
GitHub accepts all kinds: repos, code, issues.
Unit tests for research_spider tool — parallel fetches, concurrency, mixed resul
Spider with empty URL list returns empty result.
Spider fetches URLs in parallel up to concurrency limit.
Spider handles mixed ok/fail results gracefully.
Spider with dedupe=True removes duplicate URLs.
Unit tests for research_fetch tool — URL validation, caching, scrapling.  NOTE:
Fetch rejects URLs that fail SSRF validation.
Fetch result has expected fields (url, title, text, html_len, fetched_at).
Fetch returns cached result on second call (same params).
Unit tests for stealth tools — camoufox and botasaurus.
ImportError on camoufox import returns error dict.
Camoufox result includes url, title, text, tool keys.
botasaurus delegates to research_fetch with mode='dynamic'.
Unit tests for research_cache_stats and research_cache_clear.
research_cache_stats returns expected shape and values.
Stats on empty cache dir return zeros.
Stats reflect files in the cache directory.
Entries older than threshold are deleted.
Clear on empty dir returns zeros.
Unit tests for research_deep tool — search + markdown chaining.
research_deep returns dict with query, results list with url/title/markdown.
research_deep returns error when search fails.
research_deep returns partial results when markdown fetch fails.
research_deep with depth=2 limits URL fetches.
Unit tests for research_markdown tool — Crawl4AI async markdown extractor.
Inject mock crawl4ai module into sys.modules.
Test research_markdown returns correct data structure.
research_markdown returns dict with url, title, markdown, tool, fetched_at.
Test research_markdown gracefully handles Crawl4AI import failures.
research_markdown returns error when Crawl4AI unavailable.
Test research_markdown respects max character limit.
research_markdown caps markdown output at 30000 chars.
Test research_markdown rejects private IPs (SSRF protection).
Unit tests for spider tool — validation, deduplication, concurrency.
Empty URL list returns error dict.
Duplicate URLs are fetched only once.
available() returns False if OPENAI_API_KEY not set.
available() returns True if OPENAI_API_KEY is set.
chat() raises or returns error without API key.
close() is safe to call even without initialization.
available() returns False if NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY not set.
available() returns True if NVIDIA_NIM_API_KEY is set.
chat() raises or returns error without API key.
close() is safe to call even without initialization.
Unit tests for LLM provider cascade and availability logic.
Clear LLM API keys for clean testing.
All providers fail → RuntimeError with sanitized message.
available() True with API key set.
available() True with API key set.
Unit tests for loom.providers.base — cost estimation and LLMResponse.
_estimate_cost returns sensible values for known and unknown providers.
NVIDIA NIM free tier always returns 0.
OpenAI has a positive cost estimate.
Anthropic has a positive cost estimate.
Unknown providers return 0 (safe fallback, not crash).
Zero tokens → zero cost for all providers.
finish_reason defaults to None when not provided.
